title: AWS の 429 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Most factual claims are accurate and well-supported by primary AWS documentation and official blog posts.
The article's publication date is very recent (May 29, 2026), indicating high freshness.
The guidance provided aligns with AWS best practices for handling 429 errors.
One specific numeric default rate limit for a CloudWatch operation could not be independently verified from the provided search results.

One external URL cited in the article's 'Official Resources' section is a general error handling guide, and while relevant, doesn't directly provide 'specific limit values for the target service' as the article claims it does.

sources.title: Managing and monitoring API throttling in your workloads | AWS Cloud Operations Blog
sources.claim: 429 Too Many Requests is a status code returned when the frequency of calls to the AWS API exceeds the upper limit within a certain period, and AWS services have call limits (rate limits) set.

sources.title: Throttle requests to your REST APIs for better throughput in API Gateway
sources.claim: API Gateway has stage-level throttling settings, including burst and steady-state request rates, which can be configured.

sources.title: Throttle requests to your HTTP APIs for better throughput in API Gateway
sources.claim: API Gateway has stage-level throttling settings, including burst and steady-state request rates, which can be configured. AWS Support can be contacted for increasing account-level throttling limits.

sources.title: Logging AWS Lambda API calls using AWS CloudTrail - AWS Documentation
sources.claim: AWS CloudTrail can be enabled to capture API calls for analysis of timestamps and failure patterns.

sources.title: Service Quotas - User Guide - AWS Documentation
sources.claim: Service Quotas enables you to review the AWS default value and applied values of a particular quota. Service quotas can be increased by request.

sources.title: Review API calls in your AWS account using CloudTrail
sources.claim: AWS CloudTrail can be enabled to capture API calls for analysis of timestamps and failure patterns, and CloudTrail data can be viewed and tracked using CloudTrail Event history and Amazon CloudWatch Logs.

sources.title: API Rate Limiting and Throttling in AWS: Best Practices for Scalable APIs | by Nagarjun (Arjun) Nagesh | Medium
sources.claim: AWS API Gateway provides built-in rate limiting, throttling, and quotas. Stage-level throttling limits can be set.

sources.title: Request throttling for the Amazon EC2 API
sources.claim: Each AWS service has call limits (rate limits) set, and exceeding these will result in the response being rejected as a client-side error. AWS uses the token bucket algorithm to implement API throttling.

sources.title: Resolving AWS API Gateway 429 Error - Dashbird
sources.claim: API Gateway stage-level throttling settings can cause 429s. Clients may receive 429 Too Many Requests error responses when request submissions exceed steady-state request rate and burst limits.

sources.title: Monitoring and alerting on AWS service quotas | by Heeki Park - Medium
sources.claim: The Service Quotas dashboard allows for viewing quota value and utilization. The Service Quotas dashboard can be used to monitor current quota usage and set alarms.

sources.title: Resolve "ThrottlingException" or "Rate exceeded" errors in Parameter Store | AWS re:Post
sources.claim: Common error messages for 429 throttling include 'An error occurred (ThrottlingException) when calling the GetParameters operation (reached max retries: 4): Rate exceeded'. Insufficient API call interval is a cause of throttling. Implementing error retries and exponential backoff when making API calls helps prevent ThrottlingException errors.

sources.title: Resolve throttling on a DynamoDB on-demand table | AWS re:Post
sources.claim: DynamoDB tables using on-demand capacity mode can still experience throttling during certain scenarios, such as initial spikes or if maximum throughput quotas are configured and exceeded.

sources.title: How to Fix 'Rate Exceeded' and Throttling Errors in AWS - OneUptime
sources.claim: AWS applies rate limits at multiple levels, including account, service, resource, and region. Simultaneous calls from multiple processes/threads can cause spikes that lead to throttling. Exponential backoff with jitter is the standard fix for throttling.

sources.title: 429エラー（Too Many Requests）とは？原因・解決方法から予防策まで徹底解説
sources.claim: 429 Too Many Requests is an HTTP status code returned when a client exceeds the allowed number of requests within a certain period. The error message '429 Too Many Requests' or 'Rate limit exceeded' is common.

sources.title: HTTP Error 429 (Too Many Requests) - How to Fix - Postman Blog
sources.claim: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests is a client error status code indicating you've exceeded the allowed request rate. Common causes include exceeding request rate limits, concurrent request limits, and aggressive retry logic. Solutions include implementing exponential backoff and caching responses.

sources.title: ThrottlingExceptionのエラー「An error occurred (ThrottlingException) when calling the DescribeAutomationExecutions operation (reached max retries: 2): Rate exceeded」を回避するには | DevelopersIO
sources.claim: API calls without sufficient intervals, especially in loops, can cause throttling. Implementing exponential backoff is effective for retry logic.

sources.title: Logging and monitoring Service Quotas - AWS Documentation
sources.claim: AWS CloudTrail captures API calls and related events. Amazon CloudWatch monitors AWS resources in real time, allows collecting and tracking metrics, creating customized dashboards, and setting alarms. The Service Quotas dashboard can be used for real-time monitoring of current quota usage.

sources.title: DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) とインメモリアクセラレーション - AWS Documentation
sources.claim: DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) is a fully managed, in-memory cache for DynamoDB that can reduce read request frequency and improve latency.

sources.title: Requesting quota increases - AWS Resilience Hub
sources.claim: Quota increase requests are typically processed within a few business days. You can request an increase for adjustable quotas using the Service Quotas console or by contacting AWS Support.

sources.title: Request a service quota increase - AWS Support
sources.claim: If your AWS service or AWS Region isn't available in the Service Quotas console, you can create a support case to request the quota increase.

sources.title: AWS service quotas - AWS General Reference - AWS Documentation
sources.claim: Your AWS account has default quotas for each AWS service, and you can request increases for some quotas, but not all quotas can be increased.

sources.title: AWS Support Plans
sources.claim: If 429 errors persist in a production environment, contact AWS Support (Business/Enterprise plan) to request a service quota increase. Business Support and Enterprise Support plans offer technical support.

sources.title: ThrottlingExceptionのエラー「An error occurred (ThrottlingException) when calling the DescribeAutomationExecutions operation (reached max retries: 2): Rate exceeded」を回避するには | DevelopersIO
sources.claim: Common error messages for 429 throttling include 'An error occurred (ThrottlingException) when calling the DescribeAutomationExecutions operation (reached max retries: 2): Rate exceeded' and 'Rate exceeded'.

unsupported_claims: CloudWatchでは初期状態で1秒あたり最大100 Put操作 (CloudWatch defaults to a maximum of 100 Put operations per second in its initial state.)

citation_mismatches.claimed: 対象サービスの具体的な制限値を確認してください。(Check the specific limit values for the target service.)
citation_mismatches.actual: This page discusses general error handling in AWS API calls, including retry behavior and exponential backoff, rather than listing specific service limit values.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Verify the specific default rate limit for CloudWatch 'Put operations' or rephrase the claim to be more general if a precise source isn't found.

Optional improvement: Clarify the description of the 'AWS API Reference - Error Responses' URL to accurately reflect its content (general error handling) rather than implying it lists specific service limits.
Optional improvement: Add links to official documentation or primary sources for key claims.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
